description_long: This is a sleek, delta-wing military jet aircraft with a sharply pointed nose and a streamlined fuselage. The wings are broad and triangular, blending smoothly into the body, and feature roundels and insignia typical of British Royal Air Force markings. The aircraft has twin jet engines mounted at the rear, with large exhaust nozzles visible from behind. The landing gear is deployed, consisting of two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, enclosed with a clear canopy. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and a sharply swept-back design, contributing to its aerodynamic profile.
description: A delta-wing military jet with twin rear jet engines, British roundels, and a pointed nose. Features a forward cockpit, single vertical stabilizer, and deployed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the pointed nose, forward cockpit, delta wings, and deployed landing gear.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin jet engine exhausts, wide delta wings, and single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible cockpit, wing insignia, and landing gear details.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the sharp nose, cockpit canopy, and broad triangular wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-mounted delta wings with a swept leading edge and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and no horizontal stabilizers visible apart from the delta wing trailing edge.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single clear canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
